Class LimitCalculator

java.lang.Object
control.LimitCalculator
public class LimitCalculator extends Object
Calculates daily spending limits based on the remaining balance and remaining days in the active BudgetCycle.

Formula: dailyLimit = remainingBalance / remainingDays

  • Field Summary

    Description
    private DatabaseHelper
    dbHelper
    Persistence helper used to fetch spending totals and cycle data.

  • Field Details

    • dbHelper

      private DatabaseHelper dbHelper
      Persistence helper used to fetch spending totals and cycle data.

  • Constructor Details

    • LimitCalculator

      public LimitCalculator()
      Constructs a LimitCalculator backed by the singleton DatabaseHelper.

  • Method Details

    • calculateDailyLimit

      public double calculateDailyLimit(BudgetCycle cycle)
      Calculates the recommended daily spending limit for a cycle. Returns 0 if the cycle has no remaining days.
      Parameters:
      cycle - the active BudgetCycle with an up-to-date totalSpent value
      Returns:
      daily limit in EGP, or 0 if the cycle has ended

    • calculateSpentTotal

      public double calculateSpentTotal(int cycleId)
      Queries the database for the cumulative amount spent in a cycle.
      Parameters:
      cycleId - the cycle to query
      Returns:
      total spent in EGP

    • recalculate

      public double recalculate(int cycleId)
      Fetches the latest spending total, updates the cycle, and recalculates the daily limit. Called after every new expense is logged.
      Parameters:
      cycleId - the cycle to recalculate for
      Returns:
      updated daily limit in EGP
